articles.title: ‘I can out-dance Bowie and Jagger!’ Martha Reeves on Motown, Dancing in the Street and smashing crockery with Dusty Springfield

articles.description: The Indonesian musician impresses in his debut solo recording of Godowsky’s Java Suite and Stravinsky’s piano arrangement of Petrushka

more.title: Affordable Chinese brands help drive SA’s NEV transition
more.description: Almost one in five new vehicles sold in SA in Q1 2026 came from Chinese manufacturers, with a growing share being NEVs.

more.description: Review application, disputed creditor claims and missing database have plunged one of South Africa’s biggest financial scandals into fresh turmoil.

more.title: R50m from ‘Cat’ Matlala’s SAPS tender ‘paid out as bribes’, court hears
more.description: A large portion of the R50-million generated from a lucrative state tender was allegedly paid out as bribes to public officials, the court heard on Thursday as lawyers sought approval of a plea and sentence agreement with attempted murder accused businessman Vusimuzi “Cat” Matlala. The disclosure emerged during sentencing submissions after Matlala admitted his role […]

more.title: ‘Cat’ Matlala admits corruption charges in R360m SAPS tender scandal
more.description: Attempted murder-accused businessman and alleged underworld kingpin Vusimusi “Cat” Matlala on Thursday personally confirmed under oath that he freely and voluntarily admitted to dozens of allegations forming the basis of the state’s R360 million SAPS tender fraud case, a development that could significantly strengthen prosecutors’ case against the police officers and other accused who remain […]

more.title: Chomee Riding The Bafana Wave As Nation Celebrates World Cup Progress
more.description: Popular singer Chomee has joined the growing excitement around Bafana Bafana's 2026 FIFA World Cup campaign, celebrating the team's progression to the knockout stages as South Africa rallies behind Hugo Broos' men.

more.title: 1 in 10 South African families never play together, according to new LEGO® Play Well Study
more.description: 1 in 10 South African families never play together, according to new LEGO® Play Well Study Johannesburg, South Africa 25 June 2026: New findings from the LEGO® Play Well Study reveal that 92% of South African parents wish they could play more with their children, while one in ten families say they never play together [...]

more.title: South Africa become 8th African nation to reach FIFA World Cup second round in history
more.description: Bafana Bafana have written a new chapter in African football history by becoming the eighth nation from the continent to qualify for the second round of the FIFA World Cup.

more.title: SAPS and Private Security Unite Ahead of 30 June Protests
more.description: PRETORIA, SOUTH AFRICA — The South African Police Service (SAPS) and the private security industry have solidified their partnership ahead of the planned 30 June protests. Acting National Police Commissioner Puleng Dimpane confirmed that joint operations are actively in place, with authorities fully prepared to respond to any eventuality. more.title: East African Community’s expansion has triggered financial troubles: why solutions come with risks
more.description: The East African Community is one of Africa’s oldest regional economic organisations. Its birth in 1967 was the culmination of decades of economic ties forged in the colonial era between Kenya, Uganda and Tanzania. It’s no surprise that the EAC is also the most deeply integrated regional entity. In its heyday between 1967 and 1977, [...]
